qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the compatibility layer within the standard software environments?
qa_pairs.answer: The compatibility layer acts as an intermediary between the operating system and all other software packages. Its design ensures that compilers and scientific applications will function correctly, regardless of whether they are running on CentOS, Ubuntu, or Fedora.

qa_pairs.question: Which specific tools were utilized for the compatibility layer in the different versions of the standard software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: For the 2016.4 and 2018.3 versions of the standard software environment, the Nix package manager was used for the compatibility layer. In contrast, the 2020 version utilized Gentoo Prefix for this layer.
